question: What is the cladding thickness on the NuScale RPV interior?

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet all relevant regulatory requirements, such as 10 CFR 50 and its appendices. These materials are designed, constructed, and tested to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance, with specific material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4.

nuscale_ch05_0015.title: Reactor Vessel Materials
nuscale_ch05_0015.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module’s reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a containment vessel for safety. The RPV is constructed from materials specified by the ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code to ensure integrity and durability throughout the plant’s design life, serving as a critical barrier to prevent the release of fission products.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the use of ASME and NRC methodologies to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides figures and tables with these limits to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators in each NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel, forming part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary. The steam generator system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the reactor pressure vessel serving as the shell and pressure boundary for the steam generators.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a key pressure boundary to prevent the release of fission products and provides structural support for various reactor components. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant's design life.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: Threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components use Alloy 718, chosen for its compatibility with borated water and resistance to stress corrosion cracking, and comply with ASME BPVC standards. Preservice and inservice inspections and testing of pressure-retaining components follow ASME BPVC Section XI and relevant regulatory requirements, with inspection programs updated to the latest approved code editions for each inspection interval.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for materials and components used in nuclear facility applications.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the NuScale pressurizer, including the RPV upper shell, baffle plate, heater bundles, and associated components. It specifies the types of steel and nickel-based alloys used, their protective claddings, and relevant material standards (BPVC Section II) to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, operating conditions, inservice surveillance, and threaded fastener requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies procedures for maintaining vessel integrity during transport and operation, and lists the key codes and standards used for compliance.

edges.description: Section A references Section B by indicating that details of the RPV materials, which would include cladding thickness, are provided in Section 5.3, where Section B begins to describe the reactor vessel materials.

Section A establishes that RCPB materials, including those for the RPV, must comply with ASME BPVC requirements, while Section B discusses specific cladding-related welds and their compliance with relevant ASME BPVC sections, thus Section B provides detailed implementation for the general requirements stated in Section A.

Section A describes the material standards and regulatory compliance for the RPV, while Section B lists the components housed within the RPV, connecting them by specifying that the cladding thickness in question pertains to a vessel constructed per the standards outlined in Section A.

Section A describes the material standards and regulatory requirements for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, while Section B provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel as part of the system, with both sections connected by their focus on the RPV's construction and compliance with ASME and regulatory standards.

Section B references the cladding on the RPV and its examination requirements, while Section A provides the general context of the RPV but does not specify cladding thickness; thus, Section B provides details about the cladding relevant to the analysis question that Section A does not.

Section A and Section B both describe the components contained within the NuScale reactor pressure vessel (RPV), with Section A providing a general overview and Section B listing specific internal systems, but neither provides information on cladding thickness.

Section A discusses inspection and welding requirements for cladding on the RPV, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV and its components, with both sections referencing the RPV but neither specifying the cladding thickness; thus, Section A provides specific procedural details that relate to the general vessel description in Section B.
